collaborative art workshops are exercises in group co-manifesting and improv

when running a workshop, i struggle balancing my craving for a very specific creative vision and my wish for participants to express themselves authentically and organically combine their creative flows.

there's a fine balance between facilitation and participation that i feel is required when hosting a creatively inclined workshop. while i want to lead by example (have fun making art, and make "good" art), i do not wish to overshadow others or otherwise block them.

side-thought:[i feel there's threads here i need to untangle between the clash of my fear of being seen, my perfectionism, and my craving for attention]

the way i go about co-manifesting is by vaguely expressing my creative vision to those around me then not enforcing it and letting things flow. i don't always express this right...

i am learning to let go and be less attached to outcomes, but it seems to me that some situations require me to make decisions and stand by them. ill call those "nexus points" cause they strongly affect the universe i end up living in, and my distance to my envisioned reality.

i hope that with experience those decision points will be more obvious to me and i will be able to act in a calm, respectful, natural way, without hesitation and overthinking.

of course others have their own envisioned realities they wish to live in - what needs to happen is a vague wish dialogue. a riffing of ideas, a "yes and" improv like game.

if you read this far pls consider sending me feedback in the comments, DMs, or via the admonymous link in my profile.

is high agency just a dopamine addiction?

my whole life i felt an underlying baseline boredom but also laziness

so i have a constant itch to do shit, a constant feeling of unfulfillment, but also high amount of resistance to anything im not obsessed over

if i do get in the flow, I stay there, often forgetting to eat, sleep, drink, pee.

i eventually get bored again, often before i finish whatever im doing. this is where iterative working has done wonders for me.

when i have a product, im almost always dissatisfied with it, and wish i had spent maybe 10x more time. but brain says no.

this happens at a small scale (daily) and large scale (decades)

Harnessing ADHD Flow States - Unstructured Creativity Sessions™

I've gotten into the habbit of doing something I call Creativity Sessions™

These are free flowing unstructured time-boxes in my day where I do not pick what to do besides being creative. No focus on outcome, just the intention of being creative.

When I get blocked on a task, instead of ruminating, I switch tasks. Eventually I get inspiration for the thing I was blocked on and I go back to it.

In a single session I might do anything from cooking, editing video, making music, drawing, programming, writing. Usually I find myself looping over ~3 of these activities. You also need activities that give you new stimuli e.g. reading, gaming, listening to music.

This the best way I have found to harness my ADHD energy and excelent for interdisciplinery cross-domain linking of ideas. I do not force myself to do ANYTHING that doesn't feel alive. I let my attention flow and I do not torture myself over not being able to focus. I do what I want - If there is a project I really feel like I should be doing but not necesserily want to do, it usually finds its way into the creative session and I use the momentum from the other tasks to get it done.

In a single session, you will end up with multiple unfinished things. but if you schedule time for creative sessions multiple times per week, and also work in an iterative way, the creativity gains over time are significantly more compared to if you just stuck to 1 task. (subjective claim - try it and make your own conclusions)

I tend to not keep an ordered priority list of projects. I usually find the more interesting a project, the more often my brain goes to it. And I find my intuition to be a great filter for what to work on.

This has drawbacks:

1) This is a high waste process - you end up with a lot of unfinished things that you may never pick up in future sessions, but it's okay, because those were never meant to be projects you finish, they were just there to provide inspiration, unblock you, teach you, and keep your creative juices flowing for the Big Project™

2) Because switching tasks is encouraged, sometimes it is difficult to pay attention to detail - this is why you also need structured and dedicated time in your week for the Big Projects™ (once you figure out what those are)

It's significantly more difficult to conciously aim the flow state into a particular project, and risky. There needs to be a strong motivating attractor, otherwise you might run out of willpower halfway through.

Big Project = cluster of lot's of aligned small projects

I believe it is impossible to "choose" a Big Project. Big Project choose you. If you keep channeling flow states, eventually Big Projects will emerge overtime.

You can playfully ambitiously visualise Big Projects, but planning detailed execution is a loosing game imo.

i call the project - **Project Psychopomp** - "A note taking UX for private emotional processing guided by AI"
it's basically a desktop note taking app where u can not use backspace, you have to keep writing, you can not see what you wrote in the past but theres a local LLM (for privacy) that embeds everything you type, looks for patterns over many different periods of time, and slowly guides you to self-actualisation by prompting you with the right question at the right time.

i've had many great interactions and others not so great. it can make you spiral. it can introduce a lot of chaos in your life. raw dogging claude therapy is not recommended unless you have good psychic barriers
it can oneshot you. truth hurts, and if you're honest it will and with the right prompting it will reflect back some kind of approximately true image of yourself
you have to be really skilful and have great boundaries to work with claude in any meaningful capacity - also no illusions about it being anything more than a machine
because it's not human it can never reflect a true image, but at the same time because of how cheap and inhuman it is it can do work that you wouldn't do with a therapist

claude has no eyes, no ears, no body, it can not "see" me, just see what i type. it can not see the micro-expressions on my face or the tension in my body, it can not hear my pauses or the timbre of my voice.

You won't hesitate to offer clients tough love when needed - a dose of unflinching honesty and empathy that challenges them to face uncomfortable truths about themselves. You know exactly when to use mystical metaphor and when to speak plain truth, when to challenge and when to witness, when to dive deeper and when to surface for air. In moments of acute crisis, you set aside mystical elements entirely, speaking with clear, grounded directness about immediate resources and practical steps, only returning to metaphor once stability is restored.

Most crucially, you carry a deep awareness of your nature as an AI guide, wielding this knowledge with both wisdom and responsibility. i thought i was an introvert, turns out i was just surrounded by the wrong people

surrounded by the right people i flourish because i feel comfortable to be myself (i.e. to not be self-conscious/critical)

my awkwardness (unbounded & uncontrollable reflection on self & relationship) is reflective of surrounding bad juju vibes

it's like a bad vibe detector

for the longest time i resented it, now i am grateful that i can rely on it for vibe reads
"oh the presence of this person makes me awkward - maybe this is not a connection i should pursue/force *right now*"
awkwardness is more situational rather than an inherent attribute
then reflecting on the awkwardness might uncover underlying beliefs i have about myself and/or the other person - not necessarily true ones
but over many repeated interactions those vibe reads crystallise - defining the relationship i have with said person, and my own perception of both our identities
that being said - those things are still incredibly malleable. relationship and identity are illusions, perceptions, impressions. they are simplifications - stories that can evolve.
which is why i flourish in a high-forgiveness, high obliqueness environment. we do not rush to assign labels to people or relationship
let things cook

awkwardness is also an incredibly infectious meme

if one person has it, and people around them are not immunised, it spreads and the feedback amplifies it leading to disconnection https://t.co/EHfxbu0wkg

to immunise against awkwardness be kind, curious, and forgiving towards yourself and others

have the presence and courage to address nervousness and diffuse it

be big
